prevalence.value: 15-30% (older adults; higher in women and care homes)

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical assessment with bladder diary, urinalysis, and PVR when indicated; guideline-based evaluation pathways common

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: variable; assessment often integrated in geriatric and primary care
publicHealthSystem.estimatedPatients: tens of millions of older adults
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Moderate with access to pelvic floor physiotherapy and medications

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Broad access to continence services in many countries; varies by member state
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Bladder training, pelvic floor physiotherapy, medications for urge incontinence, management of BPH, and continence products support
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Service variability and long-term care demands
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Structured continence services and multidisciplinary geriatric care pathways in many systems
